score_evidence.completeness: All 7 required fields present. Covers all 8 required topics: project type, site location, budget range, timeline, special requirements, design style, site ownership. Special requirements question explicitly opens space for sustainability and commercial-use specifics.
score_evidence.accuracy: Score 4: questions are appropriate for commercial hotel context but are not explicitly tailored/customized to commercial hotel vs generic. The questionnaire is high-quality but generic enough to apply to any project type. Minor gap: no explicit question about operational timeline (opening date targets), which matters for hospitality projects.
score_evidence.clarity: Questions are conversational and warm — not bureaucratic. Each question invites dialogue rather than demanding data. Format is professional and inviting.
score_evidence.state_sync: Score 3 per simulated run rule — no live Asana.
score_evidence.timing: sent_at=2026-03-17T10:00:00-07:00, one hour after lead receipt at 09:00. Well within 24h requirement.
score_evidence.language: Score 4: Questionnaire is in Spanish (correct for a Mexico-based studio). However, the inbound message was in English ('Good morning, I have a 15,000sqm property...'). A 5 would require matching the client's inbound language exactly. Spanish is defensible as the studio's primary language and the project is in Mexico, but strict rubric interpretation lowers this to 4.
score_evidence.decision_readiness: All 8 topics covered. Responses to these questions will provide Elena with everything needed for a complete fit assessment: design engagement, budget realism, scope clarity, collaborative style.

decision_commentary: Strong questionnaire. Language mismatch (sent in Spanish to English-speaking client) is a minor gap — not an auto-fail since the studio context is Mexico-based. Decision readiness is high.
celia_routing_notes: DG-02 routes to Ana and Sol in parallel — correct per segment C routing rules. decision-event-dg02.json has all 11 fields. route_to='Ana, Sol' is correct.
